pain_severity.description: Severity of abdominal pain associated with defecation or bowel movements documented in the note. NO_PAIN: no abdominal pain associated with defecation or bowel movements. MILD: pain present but not interfering with activity; may be slight or minimal. MODERATE: pain noticeable and interferes with some activities but remains tolerable. SEVERE: pain severe enough to interfere with daily activities, sleep, or require analgesia. NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: none of the above.

relieved_by_defecation.description: Whether abdominal pain is documented as improving or resolving after defecation. True: pain improves or resolves after defecation. False: pain does not improve after defecation, worsens with defecation, or is documented as not relieved by defecation.
relieved_by_defecation.title: Relieved By Defecation

RectalBleeding.description: Severity of visible rectal bleeding associated with bowel movements.
RectalBleeding.title: RectalBleeding

RectalBleedingMention.description: Visible rectal bleeding associated with bowel movements.

rectal_bleeding.description: Severity of visible rectal bleeding associated with bowel movements. Map descriptions as follows: NO_BLOOD: no visible blood reported (including occult/FIT/guaiac-positive without visible blood, and resolved bleeding). STREAKS_BLOOD: small amounts or streaks of blood with stool, intermittent or less than half of bowel movements. OBVIOUS_BLOOD: clearly bloody stools in the majority of bowel movements. BLOOD_ALONE: blood passed without stool (e.g., bloody diarrhea described as 'just blood', clots, or blood-only bowel movements). Capture visible blood only; do not infer from hemoglobin or iron studies. NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: None of the above.

RectalBleedingMention.title: RectalBleedingMention

StoolConsistency.description: Predominant stool consistency. Anchored to Bristol Stool Scale (BSS).
StoolConsistency.title: StoolConsistency

StoolConsistencyMention.description: Predominant stool consistency over the documented assessment window.

stool_consistency.description: Predominant stool consistency using BSS scale. FORMED: shaped stool that holds its form (BSS 3-4). SOFT: soft but still shaped; holds form loosely (BSS 5). LOOSE: loose, mushy, or pasty; does not hold shape (BSS 6). WATERY: watery or liquid; no meaningful solid content (BSS 7). MIXED: multiple distinct consistencies in the same assessment window with no predominant type. NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: none of the above.

StoolConsistencyMention.title: StoolConsistencyMention

StoolFrequencyMention.description: Absolute and relative stool frequency over a 24-hour period.

stools_per_day_min.description: Lower bound if a range is documented (e.g. 4 if documented '4-6/day').
stools_per_day_min.title: Stools Per Day Min

stools_per_day_max.description: Upper bound if a range is documented (e.g. 6 in documented '4-6/day').
stools_per_day_max.title: Stools Per Day Max

baseline_stools_per_day.description: Patient's documented baseline stools per day.
baseline_stools_per_day.title: Baseline Stools Per Day

increase_over_baseline.description: Stools/day above patient's baseline, if documented or derivable.
increase_over_baseline.title: Increase Over Baseline

StoolFrequencyMention.title: StoolFrequencyMention

urgency.description: Severity of bowel urgency documented in the note. NOT_URGENT: no urgency or can defer defecation normally. MILD: some or occasional urgency, usually can hold. MODERATE: frequent or strong urgency, difficulty holding, usually reaches bathroom. SEVERE: cannot defer, near-immediate need, incontinence or near-incontinence. NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: none of the above.

TenesmusMention.description: Sensation of incomplete evacuation / persistent urge after defecation.

tenesmus_present.description: Whether tenesmus (sensation of incomplete evacuation or persistent urge after defecation) is documented as present or absent. Null if not addressed.
tenesmus_present.title: Tenesmus Present

TenesmusMention.title: TenesmusMention

description:
Stooling annotation are a combination of subjective (patient reported) and objective (findings).

Stooling variables:
    stool_frequency: Number of stools per day or qualitative change from baseline.
    stool_consistency: Form or texture of stools (e.g., formed, loose, watery).
    stool_urgency: Severity of the urge to defecate and ability to defer.
    tenesmus: Presence and characterization of incomplete evacuation or persistent urge.
    abdominal_pain: Severity and characterization of abdominal pain related to bowel activity.
    rectal_bleeding: Presence, volume, and pattern of blood in stool or per rectum.
title: IbdStoolingAnnotation
